title: Production follow-up: replace hosted pairing code with account workspace selection
description: Hosted Cloudflare OAuth still uses a static YUTOME_PAIRING_CODE and configured workspace/install/account ids. Replace hosted pairing-code consent with account-backed session/workspace selection so hosted OAuth grants represent the real Yutome user/workspace.
acceptance_criteria: Hosted OAuth consent does not require YUTOME_PAIRING_CODE; user identity and workspace are resolved from an account session or equivalent hosted auth source; grants are created for the selected workspace/user; tests cover missing session, workspace mismatch, and successful account-backed grant creation; connector-only pairing remains unchanged.

title: Review fix: redact hosted provider failure metadata
description: A review found provider exception text can be persisted to usage ledger metadata, including proxy credentials or provider key fragments. Redact sensitive tokens before hosted provider failure metadata is appended.
acceptance_criteria: Provider failure UsageEvent metadata never contains URL userinfo, bearer/API-key-looking values, proxy passwords, or obvious provider tokens from exception messages; focused provider-wrapper tests pass.
notes: Decisions relied on: provider failure text can persist to the usage ledger and must be redacted before classification/metadata, not just hidden from MCP responses. Options weighed: provider-specific redaction versus a generic hosted failure sanitizer; chose a generic sanitizer in hosted errors to cover Webshare proxy userinfo, API-key/token assignments, bearer strings, and common provider token shapes without coupling to provider clients. Files touched: src/yutome/hosted/errors.py, tests/test_hosted_provider_wrappers.py. Verification: uv run pytest tests/test_hosted_provider_wrappers.py -q passed 6/6. Follow-up risks: generic regex redaction cannot prove every future provider credential shape; provider-specific wrappers should continue avoiding raw secrets in exception messages where possible.
close_reason: Completed provider failure metadata redaction with focused provider-wrapper tests passing.

title: Production follow-up: wire real hosted entitlements into UsageGate
description: The live hosted MCP adapter currently has durable UsageGate/ledger plumbing, but default_usage_context/default_voyage_usage_context are permissive stubs when no real entitlement provider is injected. Build/read workspace entitlements and balances from Postgres so hosted query/provider calls deny by default when limits/balance are absent.
acceptance_criteria: Runtime hosted API uses a Postgres-backed UsageContextProvider for search_store and Voyage query operations; missing workspace entitlement/balance denies before provider/search calls; tests cover deny-by-default, soft/hard deny policy, and balance exhaustion; no permissive unlimited defaults in production app builder.

title: Review fix: hosted find citations and fallback semantics
description: A review found live hosted find emits youtube_url from internal video surrogate ids instead of youtube_video_id, and semantic queries silently fall back to lexical on provider/vector availability issues. Fix citation generation and make fallback behavior match the hosted retrieval contract.
acceptance_criteria: find rows prefer youtube_video_id for youtube_url while preserving internal video_id; tests use distinct internal/youtube ids; semantic provider/vector availability errors fail loudly while hybrid can fall back to lexical with explicit notes/events; targeted hosted MCP query tests pass.
notes: Decisions relied on: hosted MCP adapter owns contract-row shaping and fallback policy at the hosted boundary; SearchStore rows may carry distinct internal video_id and public youtube_video_id; provider/vector availability fallback is allowed only for hybrid because hybrid has a lexical leg, while semantic must fail loudly. Options weighed: keeping availability fallback shared by semantic/hybrid was rejected because it silently changes semantic semantics; moving URL selection into SearchStore was rejected to avoid hidden coupling outside the adapter boundary. Files touched: src/yutome/hosted/mcp_query.py; tests/test_hosted_mcp_query.py. Verification: uv run pytest tests/test_hosted_mcp_query.py tests/test_hosted_search_store.py -q => 48 passed in 0.87s. Follow-up risks: semantic vector-store availability errors currently re-raise the original store exception after recording failure, matching existing non-fallback behavior; no follow-up issue filed.
close_reason: Completed hosted MCP find citation and fallback semantics fix; targeted tests pass.

title: Review fix: fail closed hosted API and sanitize readiness
description: A review found hosted HTTP API auth fails open when YUTOME_HOSTED_API_TOKEN is unset, while /readyz can expose raw DB exception strings. Fix the live API to fail closed for tool/resource traffic by default and sanitize readiness errors without leaking connection/user/host secrets.
acceptance_criteria: Tool/resource endpoints reject requests when hosted API token is missing or invalid; health/readiness semantics remain usable for ops; /readyz exception bodies do not include raw DB URLs or credentials; focused hosted HTTP/runtime tests pass.
notes: Decisions relied on: hosted tools/resources must fail closed under the default HTTP auth dependency; missing YUTOME_HOSTED_API_TOKEN is treated as service misconfiguration (503 api_token_unconfigured), while missing/invalid bearer auth remains 401; health/readiness stay unauthenticated; explicit auth_dependency or expected_api_token keeps tests ergonomic without hidden global env coupling. Options weighed: considered preserving fail-open for local tests, rejected because it was the production vulnerability; considered returning raw readiness exception strings with redaction, rejected in favor of stable readiness error codes plus sanitizing returned check payloads. Files touched: src/yutome/hosted/http_api.py, src/yutome/hosted/runtime.py, tests/test_hosted_http_api.py, tests/test_hosted_runtime_cli.py. Verification: uv run pytest tests/test_hosted_http_api.py tests/test_hosted_runtime_cli.py -q => 33 passed in 2.67s. Follow-up risks: readiness check payload sanitization intentionally preserves short snake_case error codes only; any future rich diagnostics should be structured into non-secret fields instead of raw exception messages.
close_reason: Completed fail-closed hosted API auth and sanitized readiness error leakage; focused tests pass (33 passed).

title: Review fix: hosted Cloudflare tenant identity must not trust fallback headers
description: Claude review found Cloudflare relay routing accepts workspace/install from request headers and falls back to local/desktop under the hosted path. Split local bridge fallback from hosted mode and ensure tenant identity comes from verified OAuth/bridge credentials only.
acceptance_criteria: Cloudflare tests prove hosted mode rejects missing verified tenant identity, never trusts raw tenant headers for hosted routing, no idFromName(default), and local/BYO connector compatibility is explicit.
notes: Fixed review blocker in Cloudflare tenant resolution. Hosted mode now requires verified OAuth props for MCP routing, uses configured bridge identity for relay routing, ignores raw x-yutome-workspace-id/x-yutome-install-id headers in hosted mode, and fails before Durable Object routing when hosted tenant identity is missing. connector_only mode keeps explicit local/desktop compatibility. Verification: npm run test:ts passed 10 tests; npm run typecheck passed; rg 'idFromName("default")' cloudflare/yutome-capsule/src/*.ts returned no matches.
close_reason: Completed review blocker: hosted mode rejects missing verified tenant identity, ignores raw tenant headers for relay routing, requires OAuth props for MCP routing, and keeps connector_only local defaults explicit.

title: Review fix: hosted active transcript and search substrate contract
description: Claude review found active transcript replacement likely conflicts with partial unique index and schema does not follow plan's videos.active_transcript_version_id direction. Also SearchStore is labeled VectorChord while lexical SQL uses Postgres FTS fallback. Correct schema/query helpers and docs/tests.
acceptance_criteria: Schema and SearchStore tests use videos.active_transcript_version_id for active state; replacement SQL is safe; backend/fallback naming is explicit; tests cover VectorChord default vs Postgres FTS fallback contract.
notes:
Decisions: active transcript state now uses videos.active_transcript_version_id; transcript_versions no longer carries is_active in the hosted schema; removed the partial unique active-transcript index. Replacement SQL upserts the transcript version and updates the video pointer. Search plans join videos and only read chunks whose transcript_version_id matches the active pointer. VectorChord remains the default backend/extension check; lexical SQL is explicitly labeled postgres_fts_fallback until real vchord_bm25 SQL is implemented, with fallback metadata on lexical/hybrid usage. Options rejected: did not fake vchord_bm25 SQL; did not move indexing execution order in this ownership slice. Files touched: src/yutome/hosted/migrations.py, src/yutome/hosted/search_store.py, tests/test_hosted_postgres.py, tests/test_hosted_search_store.py, plus tests/test_hosted_indexing_smoke.py assertion updated to the new SQL contract. Tests: uv run pytest tests/test_hosted_postgres.py tests/test_hosted_search_store.py passed 13/13. Courtesy run uv run pytest tests/test_hosted_indexing_smoke.py -q now has one remaining failure in test_usage_reservation_hook_receives_operation_ids_and_stable_keys, where reservations are denied instead of reserved; not caused by this SQL contract change. Follow-up risk for Poincare indexing work: current indexing plan schedules search_store.replace_active_transcript before chunk/chunk_embedding upserts, but the plan requires building the replacement fully before swapping videos.active_transcript_version_id; indexing execution/order should adapt in its slice.
Follow-up issues created: yt-indexer-58r for Poincare/indexing active-pointer swap ordering; yt-indexer-33f for the remaining hosted indexing smoke usage-reservation denial.
close_reason: Completed hosted active transcript pointer and search substrate naming contract; targeted postgres/search_store tests pass. Force-closed despite open Phase 4 dependency because this review-fix slice is complete and follow-up issues yt-indexer-58r and yt-indexer-33f are filed.

title: Review fix: fail-closed UsageGate policy and balances
description: Claude review found UsageGate can allow calls when policy.allowed_operations and balance units are empty. Fix hosted enforcement to fail closed unless an explicit policy/balance grants the operation/units or an explicit unlimited sentinel is used.
design: Decision: UsageGate now fails closed. Empty EntitlementPolicy.allowed_operations no longer grants all operations; use allowed_operations for explicit operation grants or allow_all_operations=True for an explicit all-operation policy. WorkspaceBalance now denies any estimated unit not present in remaining_units unless that unit is listed in explicit unlimited_units. Missing balance units and over-limit balances both return insufficient_balance before provider calls.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove missing policy operation and missing balance unit deny before provider calls; existing explicit allowances remain green; denial messages are user-facing.
notes: Files changed: src/yutome/hosted/models.py; tests/test_hosted_usage.py; tests/test_hosted_provider_wrappers.py; tests/test_hosted_allocation_policy.py; tests/test_hosted_allocations.py; tests/test_hosted_gemini_integration.py; tests/test_hosted_embedding_integration.py; tests/test_hosted_webshare_integration.py; tests/test_hosted_indexing_smoke.py. Tests added/updated cover missing policy operation denial, missing balance unit denial, explicit unlimited unit allowance, and provider denied-before-call. Verification: uv run pytest tests/test_hosted_usage.py tests/test_hosted_allocations.py tests/test_hosted_allocation_policy.py tests/test_hosted_provider_wrappers.py tests/test_hosted_gemini_integration.py tests/test_hosted_embedding_integration.py tests/test_hosted_webshare_integration.py => 43 passed; uv run pytest tests/test_hosted_*.py => 101 passed. Ruff check attempted with uv run ruff check on touched files but ruff is not installed in this environment. Follow-up risk: code/tests are in an already dirty untracked hosted tree, so commit/stage boundaries need coordination with concurrent hosted broker work.
close_reason: Implemented fail-closed UsageGate policy and balance semantics with explicit operation grants/unlimited units and hosted test coverage.

title: Production follow-up: harden hosted billing money math and reconciliation
description: Billing/export schemas exist but money/credits/units still use floats and there is no authoritative balance derivation, reservation reconciliation, or webhook credit/refund executor. Build the financial layer before relying on billing as production accounting.
acceptance_criteria: Credits/money use Decimal or integer minor units; usage quantities that must be exact stay integer; balances derive from ledger/credits; reservation estimates reconcile against actuals; idempotent webhook/order processing prevents duplicate credit grants; negative credits are rejected or explicitly modeled.

title: Production follow-up: replace mock indexing smoke with real hosted ingest executor
description: hosted/indexing.py and runtime.mock_indexing_smoke are mock/dev scaffolding. Build the real hosted ingest executor that fetches transcripts, calls Gemini/Voyage through UsageGate, writes chunks/embeddings transactionally, and records terminal operation states.
acceptance_criteria: Real hosted worker executes queued public YouTube ingest jobs without SQLite/LanceDB; denied reservations short-circuit provider/search work; transcript/chunk/embedding/swap writes are transactional or replay-safe; operation states reach succeeded/failed/denied/cancelled; real test video smoke passes.

title: Review triage: separate live gaps from scaffolded hosted broker work
description: The review correctly distinguishes live query/API paths from mock/dev indexing and unbuilt billing enforcement. Audit the claims, record which are fixed now, which are scaffolding that must not be promoted, and create durable follow-up issues for real usage entitlement wiring, billing money math/reconciliation, hosted identity, and real ingest.
acceptance_criteria: Beads notes summarize true/false/partial claims; follow-up issues exist for the remaining unbuilt or scaffold-only production blockers; no markdown TODO replaces Beads.
notes: Read-only triage result: confirmed live bugs are hosted API fail-open when YUTOME_HOSTED_API_TOKEN is missing, /readyz raw exception leakage, find youtube_url using internal video_id instead of youtube_video_id, and semantic mode sharing hybrid lexical fallback. Confirmed scaffold/mock-only risks: permissive default usage contexts until real entitlement provider is wired, billing as schema/export scaffolding without accounting/reconciliation, and mock_indexing_smoke/plan_mock_hosted_public_indexing as deterministic dev scaffolding only. Designed-but-absent: account-backed hosted workspace selection instead of pairing code/static workspace, and production entitlement/balance derivation. Overstated/stale: edge hosted auth no longer trusts token props after stored-grant-authoritative fix; provider failure redaction is now centralized except readiness leakage; hybrid fallback itself is allowed, semantic fallback is the bug.
close_reason: Completed review triage: live bugs, scaffold/mock risks, absent production work, and overstated claims recorded; durable follow-ups created.

title: Phase 5 review: make hosted account grants authoritative
description: Hubble review found Worker hosted MCP validates the stored grant for active/expiry but then derives workspace/user/scopes routing from untrusted token props. Fix hosted MCP auth so the stored grant is the authority, or reject any security-relevant prop mismatch before routing.
acceptance_criteria: Hosted MCP routing uses stored active grant values or rejects prop mismatch for workspace/user/client/scopes/audience/token_version; expired/revoked behavior remains; tests cover mismatch rejection and successful stored-grant-derived routing; Worker test/typecheck pass.
notes: Decisions relied on: KV account_grants is the routing authority for hosted MCP. The token only needs to identify the stored grant; if token props include workspace_id, user_id, client_id, grant_id or connector_grant_id, scopes, audience, or token_version and any present value differs from the stored active grant, the request is rejected before hosted routing. Options weighed: deriving hosted auth entirely from token props after active grant validation was rejected because it leaves KV as only a revocation oracle; accepting token props with mismatch was rejected; requiring all props to be present was not chosen so existing signed tokens that only identify a grant can still route from stored authority. Files touched: cloudflare/yutome-capsule/src/account-grants.ts, cloudflare/yutome-capsule/src/yutome-mcp-agent.ts, cloudflare/yutome-capsule/test/pairing-oauth-props.test.ts. Verification run: cd cloudflare/yutome-capsule && npm run test:ts passed with 22 tests; npm run typecheck passed. Follow-up risks: stored grant records must remain well-formed and protected in OAUTH_KV; future token prop fields that become security-relevant should be added to the mismatch check.
close_reason: Completed: hosted MCP auth now derives routing context from active stored account grants, rejects token prop mismatches before hosted API routing, preserves revoked and expired handling, and required capsule tests/typecheck pass.

title: Phase 5 review: reconcile fallback search reservations
description: Hubble review found semantic/hybrid hosted MCP reserves search_store before embedding/vector work and then falls back to lexical without reconciling the original reservation. Fix reservation lifecycle so availability fallback does not strand quota or unclosed reservation state, while keeping UsageGate denials/auth/tenant errors non-fallback.
acceptance_criteria: Semantic/hybrid provider/vector fallback records terminal reconciliation for the original search reservation or delays reservation safely; no orphan reservation path remains; tests cover provider fallback, vector fallback, and denial/auth non-fallback behavior; targeted pytest passes.
notes: Decision: kept existing search-store denial-before-embedding behavior, rather than delaying the vector/hybrid search reservation. Added explicit terminal search-store effects after a vector/hybrid reservation exists: release when Voyage denial/failure prevents the search-store call, and failed when vector/hybrid search-store execution raises. Options weighed: delaying reservation would avoid provider-fallback releases but would allow Voyage embedding before search-store authorization denial; release/failure events preserve current authorization ordering and make fallback/non-fallback lifecycles explicit. Files touched: src/yutome/hosted/mcp_query.py, tests/test_hosted_mcp_query.py. Verification: uv run pytest tests/test_hosted_mcp_query.py -q => 31 passed in 0.14s; uv run pytest tests/test_hosted_provider_wrappers.py -q => 5 passed in 0.04s. Follow-up risks: tenant PermissionError without HTTP status is still classified as failure_kind=unknown by the existing provider classifier, but it records a terminal failed search-store event and does not fall back.
close_reason: Completed: vector/hybrid hosted MCP search reservations now receive terminal release or failed events before provider/vector fallback or non-fallback errors; focused MCP and provider-wrapper tests pass.

title: Phase 5 MCP OAuth docs cross-check
description: Read-only current-docs cross-check for the hosted Cloudflare MCP OAuth/revocation implementation. Use official MCP and Cloudflare docs to verify token props, protected resource metadata, audience/resource binding, revocation expectations, and Worker OAuthProvider shape.
acceptance_criteria: Beads notes cite official docs checked and list concrete implementation constraints/recommendations for yt-indexer-pvq.6.13; no source files edited.
notes:
2026-05-26 current-docs cross-check for hosted MCP OAuth/revocation. Read-only repo work; source files were not edited. Official docs checked via Browser/curl:
- MCP Authorization latest 2025-11-25: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/authorization
- MCP Streamable HTTP transport latest 2025-11-25: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/transports
- Cloudflare Remote MCP server guide: https://developers.cloudflare.com/agents/guides/remote-mcp-server/
- Cloudflare Securing MCP servers guide: https://developers.cloudflare.com/agents/guides/securing-mcp-server/
- Cloudflare McpAgent API: https://developers.cloudflare.com/agents/api-reference/mcp-agent-api/
- Cloudflare workers-oauth-provider README, current package metadata observed as v0.7.0 / latest release May 21 2026 on GitHub: https://github.com/cloudflare/workers-oauth-provider and raw README https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cloudflare/workers-oauth-provider/main/README.md

MUST-HAVE protocol/platform constraints for yt-indexer-pvq.6.13:
1. Hosted HTTP MCP auth is transport-level OAuth. If the Worker supports auth on Streamable HTTP, it SHOULD conform to the MCP auth spec; stdio rules are irrelevant for hosted /mcp.
2. The protected MCP server is the OAuth resource server. It MUST expose OAuth Protected Resource Metadata with authorization_servers. Current spec supports either a WWW-Authenticate resource_metadata URL on 401 or well-known metadata; clients must prefer the header when present and otherwise probe path-specific /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp before root /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource.
3. 401 challenges SHOULD include scope guidance. For Yutome's initial read-only hosted endpoint, use scope="yutome.search.read" on the Bearer challenge and expose matching minimal scopes in protected-resource / authorization metadata where supported.
4. The authorization server metadata must be discoverable. Latest MCP says AS must provide OAuth Authorization Server Metadata or OIDC discovery, and clients must support both. workers-oauth-provider implements /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server when configured with authorizeEndpoint/tokenEndpoint/clientRegistrationEndpoint.
5. MCP clients MUST send resource in both authorization and token requests. Use canonical resource https://mcp.yutome.com/mcp, including the path, because the endpoint path identifies the protected MCP resource. The Worker/resource server must validate presented tokens were issued for this resource/audience and reject tokens for other resources.
6. Access tokens must be sent in Authorization: Bearer headers on every HTTP request, even within the same logical MCP session. Never accept access tokens in query strings.
7. Invalid/expired token => 401. Insufficient scope => 403, preferably with WWW-Authenticate Bearer error="insufficient_scope", resource_metadata, and a scope parameter naming the required scopes.
8. Token passthrough is forbidden. The MCP client's Yutome token must never be accepted as a provider token or forwarded to Gemini, Voyage, Google/YouTube, Webshare, Postgres, Railway, etc. If upstream provider calls are needed, Yutome acts with its own server-side credentials/allocation path.
9. OAuth code flow needs PKCE. Current MCP latest strengthens this: clients must verify PKCE support in AS metadata; if code_challenge_methods_supported is absent clients must refuse. Configure workers-oauth-provider with allowPlainPKCE: false so metadata advertises S256-only behavior.
10. Refresh tokens, if issued to public clients, must rotate. workers-oauth-provider has refreshTokenTTL default 30 days, 0 disables refresh tokens, undefined makes them non-expiring. Its documented compromise allows two valid refresh tokens per grant at a time to survive transient client failure; do not assume strict single-valid-refresh-token semantics in app audit/reuse logic.
11. Streamable HTTP /mcp is one endpoint supporting POST and GET. POST requests must include Accept: application/json and text/event-stream; JSON-RPC notifications/responses accepted by server should return 202. Requests return application/json or text/event-stream. GET may open SSE or return 405.
12. Streamable HTTP sessions are separate from OAuth grants/tokens. If the server emits MCP-Session-Id during initialize, clients must include it on later requests; missing session can be 400, terminated session should be 404, and DELETE with MCP-Session-Id may terminate. Session IDs are not authorization and must not replace per-request Bearer validation.
13. Streamable HTTP servers must validate Origin on incoming connections to mitigate DNS rebinding; invalid Origin should be 403.
14. Current MCP 2025-11-25 adds Client ID Metadata Documents as SHOULD and treats Dynamic Client Registration as MAY / fallback/backward compatibility. This is the main delta from docs/hosted-provider-broker-plan.md, which lists POST /register as a hosted endpoint. Do not override the plan, but implementers should not rely exclusively on DCR for future MCP clients; enabling workers-oauth-provider CIMD support may be needed later and requires Cloudflare's global_fetch_strictly_public compatibility flag.

Yutome product choices that remain plan-owned, not protocol-mandated:
1. user_id, workspace_id, grant_id, client_id, scopes, audience, token_version in token props are Yutome's workspace-isolation model. MCP requires valid audience/resource/scopes, but not these exact prop names.
2. Initial scope yutome.search.read is a product scope. Protocol only requires consistent challenge/metadata/scope enforcement.
3. Revoking an OAuth grant without touching YouTube source grants/provider credentials is Yutome product policy. It aligns with the plan and token-passthrough rules, but MCP does not define Yutome's grant tables.
4. Lexical search not requiring Voyage and semantic/hybrid reserving Voyage query embedding are Yutome UsageGate/product choices, not MCP auth requirements.
5. Cloudflare Worker as edge and Railway/Postgres as query/ingest backends remain plan architecture choices.

Cloudflare Worker/library recommendations for yt-indexer-pvq.6.13:
1. Keep OAuthProvider wrapping the /mcp API handler. Cloudflare docs show either apiRoute: "/mcp", apiHandler: MyMCP.serve("/mcp") or apiHandlers: { "/mcp": MyMCP.serve("/mcp") }. McpAgent.serve handles Streamable HTTP automatically.
2. Configure OAuthProvider metadata explicitly: authorizeEndpoint "/authorize", tokenEndpoint "/token", clientRegistrationEndpoint "/register" if keeping DCR for compatibility, scopesSupported ["yutome.search.read"], allowPlainPKCE false, accessTokenTTL short, refreshTokenTTL deliberately chosen or 0 if avoiding refresh tokens in the first slice.
3. Configure resourceMetadata explicitly rather than relying on request-origin defaults: resource "https://mcp.yutome.com/mcp", authorization_servers containing the Yutome AS origin, scopes_supported ["yutome.search.read"], bearer_methods_supported ["header"], resource_name such as "Yutome MCP".
4. In completeAuthorization props include only Yutome routing/authz facts needed by the API handler: user_id, workspace_id, grant_id, client_id, scopes, audience/resource, token_version/expires if the app needs them. Do not put provider credentials, YouTube OAuth tokens, Webshare credentials, Gemini/Voyage keys, Railway/Postgres credentials, or raw upstream tokens in props.
5. Cloudflare docs say props are available on ctx.props / McpAgent props and can contain identity/tokens for external calls, but for Yutome the product constraint is stricter: no upstream provider tokens in MCP props; use server-side account/workspace allocation lookups instead.
6. workers-oauth-provider stores OAuth records in KV binding OAUTH_KV. It hashes secrets and encrypts props with token-derived material; userId and metadata are intentionally not encrypted because they support audit/revocation enumeration. Avoid putting sensitive provider material in userId/metadata; use opaque grant/user IDs.
7. Implement revocation UI/API against env.OAUTH_PROVIDER helpers. README documents listing active grants for a user and revoking/deleting a grant; deleteClient cascades and revokes all grants/tokens for that client. README does not show a first-class RFC7009 /revoke endpoint option, so the plan's POST /revoke endpoint should be treated as a Yutome app endpoint or verified against package types before assuming the library provides it automatically.
8. Schedule purgeExpiredData via a Worker cron. The library uses KV TTLs and purgeExpiredData as defense-in-depth for expired/orphaned grants/tokens; cron needs repeated batched calls because of subrequest limits.
9. Consent/authorize UI must keep CSRF/state protections. Cloudflare securing guide recommends one-time CSRF cookies, HTML sanitization for client metadata, strict CSP/frame-ancestors none, and short-lived KV state binding between consent and callback.
10. If supporting latest MCP Client ID Metadata Documents later, enable clientIdMetadataDocumentEnabled and add global_fetch_strictly_public for SSRF protection. This is not required by the existing plan's immediate DCR path but is a current-spec compatibility item.

Plan conflict/delta flags, without overriding docs/hosted-provider-broker-plan.md:
- Plan references MCP 2025-06-18 and lists dynamic client registration /register as a hosted endpoint. Latest MCP 2025-11-25 says DCR is optional/fallback and Client ID Metadata Documents are SHOULD. Recommendation: keep /register for compatibility now, but do not make it the only client registration strategy in design notes/tests.
- Plan says GET /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource or equivalent. Latest MCP is more concrete: support resource_metadata in WWW-Authenticate on 401 and either path-specific /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp or root metadata. Recommendation: Worker should provide a 401 WWW-Authenticate with resource_metadata and verify Cloudflare's default root metadata is enough for mcp.yutome.com/mcp clients; if not, add/route path-specific metadata while preserving OAuthProvider behavior.
- Plan's POST /revoke endpoint is a product requirement. Cloudflare workers-oauth-provider README documents grant revoke helpers but not an automatic /revoke route in OAuthProvider config. Recommendation: implement/verify app-level revocation using helpers; do not assume OAuthProvider exposes /revoke automatically.
close_reason: Completed read-only official-docs cross-check; notes updated with current MCP/Cloudflare constraints and plan deltas.

title: Phase 5 hosted semantic/hybrid MCP query gating
description: Wire hosted MCP semantic and hybrid find modes through Voyage query embedding and SearchStore semantic/hybrid query paths with UsageGate preflight for voyage.embed_query and search_store query operations.
acceptance_criteria: Hosted find supports semantic and hybrid modes with denial-before-embedding behavior, Voyage query usage normalization, search-store usage ledger events, provider failure classification, and tests for success/denial/failure paths; lexical mode remains provider-free.
notes:
2026-05-26 research packet for hosted semantic/hybrid MCP query gating; source untouched. Decisions relied on: docs/hosted-provider-broker-plan.md is authoritative; UsageGate/ledger enforce before provider/service work and Polar only mirrors; lexical MCP remains service_internal search_store only and provider-free; semantic/hybrid must preflight search_store.semantic_query/hybrid_query and voyage.embed_query before vector recall/provider call. Existing reusable code: src/yutome/embeddings.py:147 _embed_voyage_query already calls voyageai.Client.embed([query], input_type="query", output_dimension=dimension) and delegates hosted usage to _execute_hosted_voyage_call at src/yutome/embeddings.py:178, which wraps execute_provider_call and normalize_voyage_embeddings_response; src/yutome/hosted/normalizers.py:75 records total_tokens, vectors, output_dimension, input_type; src/yutome/hosted/provider_wrappers.py:58 reserves before call, emits provider_attempt_started/succeeded/failed, and classifies exceptions; src/yutome/hosted/errors.py:19 maps 401/403 auth, 402/quota, 429 rate_limit, 5xx transient. Search paths already exist in src/yutome/hosted/search_store.py:42 protocol and PostgresVectorChordSearchStore.semantic_search/hybrid_search at lines 127/143; semantic_query_plan/hybrid_query_plan at lines 237/269 record query_vector_dimensions, candidate limits, backend metadata. Current MCP adapter at src/yutome/hosted/mcp_query.py:167 only reserves lexical_query and calls lexical_search; HostedFindRequest rejects mode semantic/hybrid at lines 249-260.

Implementation write set to avoid conflicts with resource/read workers: own src/yutome/hosted/mcp_query.py and tests/test_hosted_mcp_query.py for this slice. Do not touch hosted resource/read/list/show/q adapters, Cloudflare Worker, HTTP API, migrations, or SearchStore SQL unless a test exposes a tiny contract mismatch. If implementation needs a new Voyage usage context hook, add it inside mcp_query.py rather than changing shared provider/search-store abstractions. Use existing _embed_voyage_query from src/yutome/embeddings.py, estimate_voyage_embeddings and estimate_search_store_query from src/yutome/hosted/allocation_policy.py. Preferred flow: parse mode into lexical|semantic|hybrid; lexical preserves current path and never constructs Voyage client/context. For semantic/hybrid, reserve search_store first so search quota denial stops before Voyage; then reserve voyage.embed_query; only after both are allowed call _embed_voyage_query; then call search_store.semantic_search or hybrid_search; append search_store service_operation_succeeded after search completes. Risk: if search_store reservation succeeds but Voyage reservation denies/fails, current event model has no explicit reservation release/cancel event; record the Voyage denial/failure and do not emit search_store success. If that orphan-reservation concern blocks durable accounting, file a follow-up for reservation cancellation semantics rather than expanding this worker's scope.

Exact tests to add/update in tests/test_hosted_mcp_query.py: (1) semantic denial-before-embedding when search_store semantic_query balance/policy denies: fake Voyage client/context must not be constructed, store.calls empty, one denied search_store event. (2) semantic denial-before-embedding when voyage.embed_query denies after search preflight: no Voyage client construction and no search_store call; ledger has denied voyage event with mcp metadata. (3) provider failure classification for Voyage query embedding: fake voyage embed raises 429 and 503 variants; ledger order is provider_attempt_started then provider_attempt_failed with failure_kind rate_limit/transient and retryable true; adapter surfaces a HostedMcpError/clear failure without calling SearchStore. (4) usage event order for semantic success: voyage provider_attempt_started, voyage provider_attempt_succeeded with actual total_tokens/vectors/input_type=query/output_dimension, then search_store service_operation_succeeded semantic_query with query_vector_dimensions/result_count/backend/index_profile metadata. (5) hybrid success mirrors semantic but calls hybrid_search(query, query_vector) and records search_store.hybrid_query candidate_limit/result_limit/query_vector_dimensions. (6) lexical provider-free behavior: mode omitted or lexical keeps hosted_find_defaulted_to_lexical behavior, never touches Voyage, only emits search_store.lexical_query; regression for unsupported semantic/hybrid rejection should be replaced. (7) search-store events: actual_units must merge usage.units plus result_count len(rows) fallback for semantic/hybrid as lexical currently does; rows map match_type/scores through _contract_find_row. Existing coverage to preserve: tests/test_hosted_search_store.py already covers semantic/hybrid SQL and usage; tests/test_hosted_embedding_integration.py already covers _embed_voyage_query usage normalization and denial; tests/test_hosted_provider_wrappers.py covers provider failure events.

Credentials/env for real-world smoke, no secrets exposed: .env.example lines 3-4 and src/yutome/cli.py lines 149-150 reference VOYAGE_API_KEY for semantic/hybrid; Voyage SDK also auto-reads VOYAGE_API_KEY. .env.example lines 22-24 and src/yutome/env.py lines 49-51 reference GEMINI_API_KEY/GOOGLE_API_KEY but those are not needed for this MCP query slice. Webshare/Youtube envs exist in .env.example lines 12-20 and CLI template lines 157-165 but are unrelated. src/yutome/hosted/auth.py lines 12-27 forbids provider credential keys, including voyage_api_key, in hosted token/auth props. Real-world smoke option after implementation: if VOYAGE_API_KEY is present in the environment, run a manually marked/single test or script that uses _embed_voyage_query(query="crohn probiotics", model="voyage-4-lite", dimension=1024, hosted_context=recording context) and asserts vector length 1024 plus normalized total_tokens; do not print the key or raw env. Official Voyage docs lookup was needed/current. Official sources: https://docs.voyageai.com/docs/embeddings says voyageai.Client.embed(texts, model, input_type, output_dimension, output_dtype) and recommends input_type query/document for retrieval; voyage-4-lite supports 1024 default and flexible dimensions including 1024. https://docs.voyageai.com/reference/embeddings-api-1 documents POST https://api.voyageai.com/v1/embeddings, input_type query/document, output_dimension, output_dtype, and returned embeddings. https://docs.voyageai.com/docs/tokenization documents Client.count_tokens(texts, model) and that VOYAGE_API_KEY is auto-used by Client. https://docs.voyageai.com/docs/pricing states text embeddings are charged by token count in documents/queries and voyage-4-lite is $0.02/M tokens after free allowance. Option weighed: using local character/token_count estimate vs Voyage count_tokens for preflight. Lexical mode remains provider-free. Provider denial maps to usage_denied without embedding/search; provider HTTP failures map to structured provider_call_failed while preserving provider_attempt_started/failed ledger events. notes: Implemented hosted MCP resource/read through a small hosted resource helper instead of folding SQL into the MCP adapter, preserving the adapter/composable shape. Decisions: resource access is exposed from PostgresVectorChordSearchStore via resource_chunk/resource_video/resource_channel/resource_transcript/resource_source; HostedMcpQueryAdapter dispatches yutome://chunk, video, channel, transcript with auth.workspace_id only; missing and cross-workspace reads both surface as resource_not_found 404; show is implemented minimally for chunk/video/channel/transcript/source and returns unsupported_show_context for context expansion. Options weighed: avoided SQLite/LanceDB compatibility and avoided billing/usage events for resource reads; kept existing lexical find usage reservations and ledger behavior intact. Files touched: src/yutome/hosted/resources.py, src/yutome/hosted/search_store.py, src/yutome/hosted/mcp_query.py, tests/test_hosted_mcp_query.py, tests/test_hosted_http_api.py, tests/test_hosted_search_store.py. Verification: uv run pytest tests/test_hosted_mcp_query.py tests/test_hosted_http_api.py tests/test_hosted_search_store.py -q passed 41 tests. Risks: channel resources are summary rows inferred from hosted videos/sources metadata because there is not a separate hosted channels table; context expansion remains intentionally unsupported in this slice.
close_reason: Completed hosted MCP resource/read and minimal show parity with workspace-scoped Postgres resource helpers; targeted hosted MCP/API/search-store tests pass.

Decisions: staged adapter stores user/workspace/client/scopes/audience/token_version/status timestamps in OAUTH_KV under yutome:account-grant:<grant_id>; provider credentials are not included in props or metadata; connector_only keeps yutome-owner compatibility. - Worker -> API bearer enforcement: HostedMcpApiClient requires YUTOME_HOSTED_API_TOKEN; Python http_api verifies Authorization: Bearer when configured.
- Python hosted MCP query API exists: src/yutome/hosted/mcp_query.py and http_api.py; runtime.build_hosted_api_app wires Postgres SearchStore, UsageGate, ledger; cli.py exposes yutome hosted api.
- Query-side lexical usage exists for find: HostedMcpQueryAdapter reserves search_store.lexical_query, scopes by workspace_id from auth, appends usage event metadata including mcp user/grant/client/session.

Remaining Phase 5 blockers, code-required:
1. Account-backed OAuth is not implemented end-to-end. /authorize still uses the pairing form and completeAuthorization userId "yutome-owner" in cloudflare/yutome-capsule/src/pairing.ts; props are enriched from configured tenant/env values, not a real Yutome account session, workspace chooser, persisted MCP grant, refresh rotation, or revocation-aware control-plane record. This blocks the plan requirement to replace printed local pairing as the primary hosted flow and store grants by Yutome user/workspace/client.
2. Hosted contract parity is incomplete. Python HostedMcpQueryAdapter only supports find with lexical mode over chunks; list/show/q, semantic/hybrid modes, filters/offset, non-chunk targets, and all resource reads return unsupported/501. Cloudflare exposes contract.json parity, but hosted API implementation does not yet satisfy find/list/show/q/resource-read parity.
3. Hosted resource reads are not implemented. http_api has /resources/read and Worker dispatches to it, but mcp_query.SUPPORTED_RESOURCE_HOSTS is empty and read_resource always raises unsupported_resource. This is explicit Phase 5 acceptance/product behavior gap.
4. Semantic/hybrid query execution and Voyage embed_query gating are not wired through the MCP query API. search_store.py has semantic/hybrid SQL plans, but mcp_query.py rejects non-lexical mode and never calls Voyage or reserves voyage.embed_query.
5. OAuth revocation/audit/token lifecycle are only data-model-adjacent, not live endpoints. Worker OAuthProvider config exposes /token and /register; no explicit /revoke route or account-backed revocation/audit flow was found. Hosted control_plane/migrations model grant statuses/revoked_at/token_version, but the Cloudflare hosted OAuth flow does not consult or mutate those records.
6. Live Railway/API deployment is not proven by this audit. Local CLI/app wiring exists, but yt-indexer-pvq.6.9 is still in_progress and no live hosted API URL/readiness was evidenced here.
7. Production Worker config is still connector_only in wrangler.toml and the parent notes record production workers.dev health as connector_only. Hosted staging exists by Wrangler deployment metadata, but custom domain and production routing are not accepted.

Remaining Phase 5 blocker, external/infrastructure:
- https://mcp.yutome.com/mcp is not reachable because mcp.yutome.com has no A/CNAME DNS answer. This blocks the explicit acceptance criterion "MCP clients connect to https://mcp.yutome.com/mcp" independent of code. Need Cloudflare zone/domain routing ownership or DNS/custom-domain setup.

Non-blocking/post-V1 follow-ups or lower priority once blockers clear:
- Add OpenAI-compatible search/fetch aliases only as additive distribution aliases; do not rename existing Yutome tools.
- Capability metadata for incomplete hosted mode should be explicit until full parity ships. Current unsupported errors are explicit, but user-facing capability discovery can be improved.
- Bridge heartbeat/offline replica metadata is partially represented in tenant-routing helpers, but full bridge status UX can follow after account OAuth and hosted API routing are real.

Options weighed:
- Do not close Phase 5 parent: external DNS plus code-required OAuth/parity/revocation gaps still violate acceptance.
- Treat lexical hosted find as a good intermediate slice: tests pass and usage/workspace isolation are real, but it is not enough for Phase 5 acceptance.
- Next best code slice after in-progress child work: account-backed OAuth/revocation path or resource/show parity, depending on whether live domain/API deployment is unblocked first.

Recommendation:
- Keep yt-indexer-pvq.6 open. Close only this audit task. Label mcp.yutome.com routing as external/infrastructure; label OAuth account backing, revocation/audit, hosted API deployment, and contract/resource parity as code-required.
close_reason: Read-only spec-gap audit completed and notes updated with blockers/evidence

Decisions: derive workspace_id/install_id from verified bridge identity; use the pairing auth request id for connector_grant_id and grant_id until account-backed grant persistence exists; derive client_id and scopes from state.authRequest; use env.YUTOME_MCP_AUDIENCE when set, otherwise request-origin /mcp; use env.YUTOME_TOKEN_VERSION when set, otherwise v1; include expires_at using env.YUTOME_TOKEN_TTL_SECONDS when valid, otherwise a 30-day default. title: Define per-call hosted Gemini idempotency for fan-out
description: Hosted Gemini transcription and cleanup can fan out into multiple generate_content calls. The Phase 1 Gemini slice accepts one ProviderCallContext and applies it to each request, preserving opt-in behavior and denial enforcement. A hosted control-plane follow-up should decide whether callers must synthesize per-window/per-batch idempotency keys, estimates, and metadata for reconciliation.
notes: Implemented per-call hosted Gemini context derivation for fan-out. Decisions relied on: UsageGate/Yutome ledger remain the pre-call authorization source; provider wrappers stay opt-in by only deriving contexts when ProviderCallContext is supplied; retries must get deterministic keys that are stable across job reruns but distinct for additional paid generate_content attempts. Options weighed: reusing the parent operation key was rejected because multi-window/multi-batch calls collide in reservations/events; moving logic into hosted wrappers was avoided to keep wrappers generic and preserve explicit caller state/effects; arbitrary token-estimate splitting was avoided because this slice lacks per-batch count_tokens data. Transcription windows now suffix parent idempotency with window identity and attach window metadata, deriving media_seconds only when exact bounds are known. Cleanup batches now suffix by batch/sequence window, and validation retries suffix by attempt. Files touched: src/yutome/gemini.py, src/yutome/quality_llm.py, tests/test_hosted_gemini_integration.py. Verification: uv run pytest tests/test_hosted_gemini_integration.py tests/test_hosted_provider_wrappers.py tests/test_hosted_usage.py -q passed (26 passed). Ruff file-scope check attempted but skipped because ruff is not installed in the environment. Residual risks: cleanup token estimates are still inherited from caller context rather than recomputed per batch; full no-duplicate-provider-result replay after a successful provider response remains a later durable operation-state concern outside these files.
